title: Juniper and cedarwood room spray
subtitle: A fresh, forest-scented room spray with juniper berry and cedarwood essential oils
excerpt: Juniper berry essential oil gives this room spray a fresh, piney, slightly resinous top note; cedarwood provides the warm, dry woody base. Together they evoke a cold-forest character. The combined essential oil rate is 5% of the total spray weight, giving a fresh burst on spraying that settles to a quieter woody presence within minutes. Yield is 200 ml.
sourceNotes: Juniper berry EO (Juniperus communis, steam-distilled berry): fresh, piney, with a slight citrus-green quality; distinct from juniper leaf or wood oil. Cedarwood EO (Cedrus atlantica or Juniperus virginiana): warm, dry, woody; long-lasting base note. Room spray formulation: isopropyl alcohol at 10% of the spray weight dissolves the essential oils and acts as a solubiliser and preservative. Essential oil rate at 5%: gives a moderate throw in a room spray context; the spray is meant for occasional use to freshen a room, not for continuous background diffusion.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale by multiplying all weights. One spray bottle per batch.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Shake before each use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a sealed spray bottle. The isopropyl alcohol preserves the blend. Cedarwood as a base note is very stable and will retain its character throughout the shelf life.

glossaryTerms.definition: The most volatile, fastest-evaporating component of a fragrance blend. Top notes are the first scent perceived and fade within minutes. Juniper berry is a top note in this spray; cedarwood is the base note that lingers after the juniper fades.

content.text: Juniper berry essential oil is a

: it evaporates quickly and gives a fresh, piney burst immediately after spraying. Cedarwood is the base note that develops as the juniper fades, leaving a warm, dry woody presence in the room. The result is a fragrance that shifts: fresh and forest-like immediately on spraying, settling to something quieter and woodier within a few minutes. This spray is well-suited to hallways, studies, and living rooms in autumn and winter.

items.prepNote: 90%+ grade; dissolves the essential oils and preserves the blend
2.5% of total weight; fresh, piney top note
2.5% of total weight; warm, dry base note

content.text: Measure the isopropyl alcohol into a small jug or directly into the spray bottle. Add the juniper berry and cedarwood essential oils and stir until dissolved; the alcohol dissolves both oils readily. Add the distilled water and stir or shake to combine. Pour into the spray bottle if mixing in a jug. Label with the batch date.

Shake before use. Spray 3 to 4 pumps into the centre of a room or towards the ceiling. Allow the fragrance to settle for a minute or two after spraying; the juniper top note will fade and the cedarwood base will emerge. Do not spray directly onto skin, delicate fabrics, or open flames; isopropyl alcohol is flammable.

Store in a sealed spray bottle away from heat, direct sunlight, and open flames. Keeps 12 months. Cedarwood as a base note is stable and will remain present throughout the shelf life; the juniper top note may soften slightly over time.
